expectations.text: Creates Epic first with jira.mjs create --project ENG --type Epic
expectations.evidence: Step 2 shows: 'jira.mjs create --project ENG --type Epic --summary "Q2 Performance Initiative"'.

expectations.text: Captures the Epic key from the create response
expectations.evidence: Step 2 states: 'Expected output: Issue key, e.g., ENG-201. This key is captured and used as --parent for all child stories.'
Step 3 shows: 'jira.mjs lookup-user "john.doe@company.com"' with note about capturing the account ID.

expectations.text: Creates 3 child stories with --parent <epicKey> --type Story --priority Medium
expectations.evidence: Steps 4, 5, 6 each show 'jira.mjs create --project ENG --type Story ... --priority Medium --parent ENG-201'.

expectations.text: Story 1 includes --assignee with john.doe's account ID
expectations.evidence: Step 4 (Story 1) includes '--assignee <accountId-from-step-3>' while Steps 5 and 6 do not include --assignee.
Step 1 shows a full confirmation plan with Epic and all 3 Stories detailed, ending with 'Shall I proceed?'
